svg.text: ASV Catamaran · Pool test #1 fixed: Week 9 (Jul 20) · Read left column top→bottom, then right column top→bottom
280mm mast · 165mm bow brace · deburr all cuts
Dry-fit everything before any adhesive touches PVC
JB Weld MarineWeld on all 4 end caps
Marine silicone secondary bead around each joint
Submerge + blow air — zero bubbles required before proceeding
⏱ 24-HOUR CURE GATE — do not proceed until end caps are fully set
Position cross members at 150 / 350 / 550mm from bow
Mark + drill M4 bolt holes through pontoon walls
Check square (equal diagonals) before drilling — no glue yet
JB Weld + marine silicone at all cross-member contact points
L-brackets + M4 bolts · hose clamp at each pontoon junction
Re-check square while curing · brace until fully set
⏱ 24-HOUR CURE GATE — hull bond must fully set before deck load
440×314mm marine ply or Al sheet · seal all edges first
Bolt through cross members · M3 standoffs at 160×160mm pattern
Enclosure footprint centered on deck — see top-view diagram
Bow brace at deck level (CM1 face to mast foot) · mast vertical
Mast top 250–300mm above waterline · zip-tie coax every 50mm
Route RX coax from transducer down mast into enclosure gland
JB Weld thruster mount + silicone seal at pontoon gland
Motor leads through cable gland · cure 24hr before water contact
⚠ Stall current (5–8.6A) destroys L298N — firmware trip required first
🔴 BENCH TEST GATE — stall current measured + thrust ≥150g confirmed + firmware trip verified
Bare hull in pool/tub · mark actual waterline on each pontoon
Check bow/stern trim · repeat loaded with ~400g dead weight
Target: deck ≥60mm above WL · adjust cross-member height if needed
7× M16 glands (motors×2, JSN, coax, LiPo, UART, spare)
Load electronics · torque glands · IP67 gasket seated all sides
Marine silicone bead on each external gland ring as backup
PV-1/2/3 on AD9226 · buck output 4.9–5.1V · vcgencmd = 0x0
ROS 2 nodes + UART 8-byte packet verified · star ground confirmed
All checks pass → ready for pool test #1 (Week 9, Jul 20)
✅ Hull complete — proceed to pool test #1 (Week 9, Jul 20)
